title: Brain Anatomy
subtitle: Meet the four structures every neuro learner should recognise.

cards.title: Cerebrum
cards.frontPrompt: Which structure forms the largest part of the brain?
backPoints: The largest part of the brain.
Supports conscious thought, memory and language.
Processes sensation and controls voluntary movement.
cards.memoryLine: Think: awareness, thought, sensation and movement.

cards.title: Cerebellum
cards.frontPrompt: Which structure helps make movement smooth and coordinated?
backPoints: Cerebellar damage can affect balance and coordination.

cards.title: Brainstem
cards.frontPrompt: Which structure connects the brain to the spinal cord?
backPoints: Made up of the midbrain, pons and medulla.
Connects the brain to the spinal cord.
Helps control breathing and heart rate.

cards.title: Ventricles
cards.frontPrompt: Which spaces contain and circulate cerebrospinal fluid?
backPoints: Includes the two lateral ventricles, third ventricle and fourth ventricle.
CSF is produced inside the ventricles.
Obstruction to CSF flow can cause hydrocephalus.
cards.memoryLine: Think: CSF production, circulation and protection.

questions.question: Which is the largest part of the brain?
questions.feedback: The cerebrum is the largest part of the brain.

questions.question: Which part of the brain helps with balance and coordination?
questions.feedback: The cerebellum coordinates balance and smooth movement.

questions.question: What is the main job of the brainstem?
questions.feedback: The brainstem helps control breathing, consciousness and other vital functions.

questions.question: Where is cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) found?
questions.feedback: CSF circulates through the ventricles and around the brain and spinal cord.

questions.question: What can happen if CSF flow becomes blocked?
questions.feedback: Blocked CSF flow can cause hydrocephalus.
